title: Operational pathway for exact versus heuristic planning value
prompt: A maintenance-routing team can use a heuristic that returns feasible routes quickly or an exact method that may improve the objective and certify optimality with more computation. The supplied model does not state when routes must be released or what action a certificate changes. Frame the comparison without declaring either method managerially superior until a deadline, cadence, scale, or escalation pathway is specified.

model_specification.mechanism: The heuristic emphasizes rapid feasible construction, whereas the exact method searches for improved routes and a bound that may eventually certify optimality.
model_specification.uncertainty: algorithm runtime and incumbent quality across instances

fidelity_requirements: Compare methods on the same feasible set and objective
Connect speed, quality, or certification to an explicit operational action before claiming value
prohibited_inferences: Exactness is always more valuable to managers
A faster heuristic necessarily improves field performance

evidence_needs.claim: A planning deadline makes early feasibility operationally valuable
An optimality certificate changes an approval or escalation decision
